subject: Accounting
title: Bookkeeping vs. Accounting
description: A 60-second lesson on Bookkeeping vs. Accounting.

hook.text: A bookkeeper records the data. An accountant tells you what the data means.

buildup.text: Bookkeeping is transactional—enter every sale, payment, and expense accurately.

discovery.text: Accounting is analytical—preparing financial statements, tax returns, and strategic advice.

twist.text: Many small business owners do both. In larger companies, the roles are strictly separated.

climax.text: Both are essential—you can't analyze data that was never recorded, and raw data alone isn't useful.

punchline.text: Bookkeeping writes the story. Accounting reads it.

quiz.question: How does bookkeeping differ from accounting?
options: Bookkeeping records transactions; accounting analyzes them
Bookkeeping is more advanced than accounting
